index.heading: Ambitecture
index.text:
Ambitecture lets you design light and color in space, then perform it live.

Start here:
• [The stage](stage.index) — where you place and play your lights
• [Intents](intent.index) — the spatial "wishes" that drive the lights
• [Scenes](scene.index) — saved looks you switch between

For real hardware (DMX, LED…) a matching [renderer](renderer) must be connected.

Tip: keep this panel's "Auto" toggle on and help will follow what you click. Turn it off for silence. Made by [Kobald](https://kobald.com).

no-help-available.heading: Ooops!
no-help-available.text: No help is available for this topic.

renderer.heading: Renderer

heightSlider.heading: Height
heightSlider.text: Sets how high off the floor this element sits. On the stage you can also drag the small vertical handle next to it to change height directly.

stage.index.heading: The Stage
stage.index.text:
The stage is your room seen from above — a space where you place lights (intents) and see your fixtures. It works in three modes, shown as tabs:

• [Perform](stage.perform) — run the show live
• [Edit](stage.edit) — build and arrange your lights
• [Scenes](scene.index) — save and switch between looks

The hub takes everything you place here and works out, for every physical fixture, what color and brightness it should show.

stage.perform.heading: Perform
stage.perform.text:
Live mode. Here you play the show in real time: drag enabled lights around the room and tweak them with knobs, and the change goes out to your fixtures instantly.

Moves in Perform are temporary — they ride on top of the saved scene and don't change it. Use [Reset scene](stage.perform.resetScene) to snap back.

Controls: [HUD knobs toggle](stage.perform.hudToggle) · [knobs](stage.perform.knob). Only lights you marked in [Perform Enable](stage.edit.performEnable) can be moved here.

stage.perform.hudToggle.heading: Show / hide knobs
stage.perform.hudToggle.text: Shows or hides the round control knobs that float over each light on the stage. Hide them for a clean view; show them to adjust lights live.

stage.perform.resetScene.heading: Reset scene
stage.perform.resetScene.text: Clears the live tweaks you've made in Perform and returns everything to the saved scene. Your scene on disk is never changed by performing — this just throws away the temporary changes on top of it.

stage.perform.knob.heading: Live knobs
stage.perform.knob.text:
Each knob adjusts one property of one light in real time — brightness, radius, strobe, and so on. Turn it and the fixtures update immediately.

You choose which properties get a knob with the ❂ button in the intent editor ([quick panel](intent.edit.quickPanel)).

stage.edit.heading: Edit
stage.edit.text:
Build mode. Here you place lights, arrange them, and set their properties — changes are saved to the project.

In Edit the fixtures are locked and the physics is paused so nothing drifts while you work.

• Double-tap empty space to [add a light](stage.edit.createIntent)
• Double-tap a light to open its [editor](intent.edit)
• Drag a light to move it

Toolbar: [Fixtures lock](stage.edit.fixtureLock) · [Perform Enable](stage.edit.performEnable) · [Perform sort](stage.edit.performSort) · [Select](stage.edit.select) · [Physics](stage.edit.physics).

stage.edit.fixtureLock.heading: Lock / unlock fixtures
stage.edit.fixtureLock.text: Fixtures are locked by default so you don't move them by accident while arranging lights. Unlock (🔓) to drag the physical fixtures into their real-world positions; lock again (🔒) when you're done.

stage.edit.performEnable.heading: Perform Enable
stage.edit.performEnable.text: Choose which lights the operator is allowed to grab and move during the live show. Tap a light's circle to switch it on (blue) or off. Only enabled lights can be dragged or knob-controlled in Perform. Tap Done when finished.

stage.edit.performSort.heading: Perform sort
stage.edit.performSort.text: Sets the left-to-right order of the buttons on your Perform control strip. Drag the rows to reorder, then close — the new order is saved.

stage.edit.select.heading: Select
stage.edit.select.text: Tap lights to select one or several at once; their shared editor opens so you can change them together. Tap empty space to clear the selection.

stage.edit.physics.heading: Physics
stage.edit.physics.text: Turns the hub's physics solver on or off while editing. With physics on, lights linked by connections (rods, springs, ropes) pull on each other when you drag one. It's off by default in Edit so positions stay put; in Perform it's always on.

stage.edit.createIntent.heading: Add a light
stage.edit.createIntent.text:
You added a new element to the stage. Pick its type:

• Light — emits color and brightness around a point
• Master — a global dimmer / blackout over everything
• Target — a magnet that aims movable fixtures

The new element joins the current scene. Double-tap it to open its [editor](intent.edit). See [intents](intent.index) for what each type does.

intent.index.heading: What is an intent?
intent.index.text:
An intent is a "wish" for light at a place in the room — for example "warm amber glow here, about 5 metres wide". You never address a specific lamp; you describe what you want, and the hub figures out what every nearby fixture should do to make it happen.

Three kinds:
• Light — color, brightness and spread at a position
• Master — a global brightness / blackout over everything
• Target — aims movable fixtures toward or away from a point

Place intents in [Edit](stage.edit), tune them in the [intent editor](intent.edit), and group them into [scenes](scene.index).

intent.edit.heading: Intent editor
intent.edit.text:
This panel edits the selected light. Each row is one property with its value control; some rows have small buttons on the left to switch options.

• [Color](intent.edit.color) · [Radius](intent.edit.radius) · [Alpha](intent.edit.alpha) · [Blend](intent.edit.blend) · [Strobe](intent.edit.strobe)
• Row buttons: [On/Off](intent.edit.toggle) · [❂ quick knob](intent.edit.quickPanel) · [Shared/Overlay](intent.edit.overlay)
• [Connections](connections.index) link lights together

Top/footer: [× close](intent.edit.close) · [Copy](intent.edit.copy) · [Delete](intent.edit.delete).

intent.edit.close.heading: Close
intent.edit.close.text: Closes the editor and deselects the light. Nothing is lost — your edits are already saved as you make them.

intent.edit.copy.heading: Copy
intent.edit.copy.text: Duplicates the selected light (or lights) into the current scene, dropped slightly offset so you can see the copy. Handy for repeating a look across the room.

intent.edit.delete.heading: Delete
intent.edit.delete.text: Removes the selected light. You'll be asked whether to remove it just from this scene (it still exists elsewhere) or delete it completely from the project.

intent.edit.toggle.heading: On / Off
intent.edit.toggle.text: Switches an optional property on or off. Off means "don't set this" — the light leaves that property alone and other lights or defaults decide it. On sets it to a value you can then adjust. "Mix" appears when several selected lights disagree.

intent.edit.quickPanel.heading: ❂ Quick knob
intent.edit.quickPanel.text: Adds this property's knob to the Perform screen so you can adjust it live during the show. Tap again to remove the knob. Only some properties (like brightness, radius, strobe) can have a knob.

intent.edit.overlay.heading: Shared or Overlay
intent.edit.overlay.text: Decides whether this value belongs to the light everywhere (Shared) or is an override just for the current scene (Overlay). Use Overlay when one scene needs, say, a redder color while every other scene keeps the shared one. "Mixed" appears when a multi-selection disagrees.

intent.edit.inputAssign.heading: Assign an input
intent.edit.inputAssign.text: Links a physical control — a button, key or MIDI knob — to this light, so hardware can trigger or drive it without touching the screen.

intent.edit.performReset.heading: Perform reset
intent.edit.performReset.text: Picks which of this light's values snap back to their saved setting when you clear your live Perform tweaks. Turn a value on here if a temporary change to it should be undone by Reset scene.

intent.edit.color.heading: Color
intent.edit.color.text:
Pick a color from the palette (hue, saturation, lightness). Behind the scenes the hub stores it in a device-independent form (CIE xyY — basically "which color" plus "how bright") and then maps it to whatever each fixture can actually produce, so the same color looks right across very different lights.

If several lights are selected, the sliders nudge every light's color by the same amount instead of forcing them all to one color.

intent.edit.alpha.heading: Alpha (intensity)
intent.edit.alpha.text: How strongly this light contributes — its overall intensity / opacity in the blend with other lights. Lower it to fade the light back without changing its color.

intent.edit.blend.heading: Blend
intent.edit.blend.text: How this light mixes with others that overlap it: Add (colors pile up and brighten), Alpha (normal layering by intensity), or Multiply (darkens / tints what's underneath).

intent.edit.radius.heading: Radius
intent.edit.radius.text: How far the light reaches from its position, in metres. Fixtures inside the radius pick it up; the [falloff curve](intent.edit.radiusFunction) shapes how it fades toward the edge.

intent.edit.radiusFunction.heading: Falloff
intent.edit.radiusFunction.text: The curve that controls how the light fades from full at the centre to nothing at the edge of its radius — a soft gradient or a harder edge.

intent.edit.strobe.heading: Strobe
intent.edit.strobe.text: Makes the light flash. Higher values flash faster; zero is steady.

intent.edit.amber.heading: Amber
intent.edit.amber.text: An extra warm-amber channel for fixtures that have one, blended in on top of the main color for a warmer feel.

intent.edit.mass.heading: Mass
intent.edit.mass.text: How heavy the light feels when dragged with physics on — heavier lights lag and swing more, lighter ones snap to your finger.

intent.edit.drag.heading: Drag
intent.edit.drag.text: How much the light resists motion under physics — more drag settles it quickly, less drag lets it drift and overshoot.

intent.edit.position.heading: Position
intent.edit.position.text: Where the light sits in the room (X across, Y up, Z depth). You'll usually set this by dragging on the stage rather than typing.

intent.edit.freeze.heading: Freeze axis
intent.edit.freeze.text: Locks movement on the X, Y or Z axis so physics and dragging can't change that direction — useful to keep a light on a line or at a fixed height.

intent.edit.layer.heading: Layer
intent.edit.layer.text: Stacking order for blending. Higher layers sit on top of lower ones when lights overlap.

intent.edit.name.heading: Name
intent.edit.name.text: A label for this light so you can recognise it in lists and on the stage.

intent.edit.heightSlider.heading: Height handle
intent.edit.heightSlider.text: Turns on the little vertical handle on the stage so you can drag this light's height directly.

intent.edit.brightness.heading: Master brightness
intent.edit.brightness.text: The global dimmer. This Master intent scales the brightness of everything at once — pull it down to dim the whole room, to zero for blackout.

intent.edit.mode.heading: Attract / Repel
intent.edit.mode.text: For a Target: Attract pulls movable fixtures to aim toward this point; Repel pushes their aim away from it.

intent.edit.followTime.heading: Follow time
intent.edit.followTime.text: How quickly fixtures swing to follow this Target. Short times snap; longer times glide smoothly.

connections.index.heading: Connections
connections.index.text: A connection physically links two lights so they move together under physics — drag one and the other follows. Choose the [kind](connections.kind) (rod, spring or rope) and tune its [strength](connections.param). Add one with + Connect, remove it with 🗑 ([release](connections.release)).

connectIntent.heading: Connect intents
connectIntent.text: Tap an intent on the stage to connect it to this one. Tap empty space, or × here, to cancel. See [connections](connections.index).

connections.kind.heading: Connection kind
connections.kind.text: Rod holds a fixed distance (stiff), Spring pulls back toward a rest length (bouncy), Rope only pulls when stretched taut (slack otherwise). Each gives a different feel when you drag linked lights.

connections.param.heading: Connection strength
connections.param.text: Tunes the linked behaviour — for example how stiff the rod's rotation is, or how strong the spring's pull is.

connections.release.heading: Release
connections.release.text: Removes this connection. The two lights move independently again.

scene.index.heading: Scenes
scene.index.text:
A scene is a saved look — a chosen set of lights with their settings. Switching scenes recalls that look instantly; only the active scene is sent to your fixtures.

A scene can also override specific values just for itself ([Overlay](intent.edit.overlay)) while sharing the rest.

• [Recall](scene.recall) a scene by tapping it
• [Rename](scene.rename) · [Copy](scene.copy) · [Delete](scene.delete)
• [Add/remove lights](scene.intentToggle) from a scene
• [Assign](scene.performAssign) hardware to recall a scene

To save the current live state instead, use a [snapshot](snapshot.index).

scene.recall.heading: Recall scene
scene.recall.text: Makes this scene active. Its look goes out to the fixtures right away, and any temporary Perform tweaks from the previous scene are cleared.

scene.rename.heading: Rename scene
scene.rename.text: Gives the active scene a new name so it's easy to find in the list and on your Perform buttons.

scene.copy.heading: Copy scene
scene.copy.text: Creates a new scene that starts as a duplicate of this one — a quick base for a variation without rebuilding from scratch.

scene.delete.heading: Delete scene
scene.delete.text: Removes this scene. You'll be asked to confirm; you can't delete the last remaining scene.

scene.intentToggle.heading: Lights in this scene
scene.intentToggle.text: Switches a light in or out of this scene. A scene only sends the lights it includes, so use these toggles to decide what the look contains.

scene.performAssign.heading: Assign to recall
scene.performAssign.text: Maps a physical input (button, key, MIDI) or a pulse to recalling this scene, so you can trigger the look from hardware during the show.

snapshot.index.heading: Snapshots
snapshot.index.text:
A snapshot captures the current live state — the active scene plus running pulses and animations — so you can jump straight back to it later. Unlike [scenes](scene.index), which you build, snapshots grab whatever is happening right now.

• [Create](snapshot.create) a snapshot
• [▶ Play](snapshot.play) to recall it
• [Modify](snapshot.modify) · [Delete](snapshot.delete)

snapshot.create.heading: Create snapshot
snapshot.create.text: Saves the current live state. Name it and choose what to capture — the scene, running pulses, and/or animations — then save.

snapshot.play.heading: Play snapshot
snapshot.play.text: Recalls this snapshot, restoring the captured state live.

snapshot.modify.heading: Modify snapshot
snapshot.modify.text: Rename a snapshot, change what it captures, or re-grab the current state into it.

snapshot.delete.heading: Delete snapshot
snapshot.delete.text: Removes this snapshot. The scenes and lights it referenced are untouched.
